query.sentence:
Beyond PEEK, which thermoplastic materials can:
- Withstand continuous or intermittent use up to 200 °C
- Offer good chemical compatibility with acid, bases, organic solvents, oils
- Be co extruded with softer polymers such as TPU, TPE, or TPV (e.g., in multilayer or jacketed constructions)
query.application_Y: Withstand 200°C, good chemical compatibility with acid, bases, organic solvents, oils, co extrusion with softer polymers

properties: continuous operating temperature ≥200 °C (or intermittent up to 250 °C)
chemical resistance to strong acids, bases, organic solvents and oils
good melt flow for extrusion (MFR 0.5–10 g/10 min at 230 °C)
compatibility with co‑extrusion of elastomers such as TPU, TPE or TPV
constraints: melt temperature must be compatible with the lower‑temperature elastomer (≤220 °C) to avoid degradation
viscosity mismatch between hard and soft layers requires a compatibilizer (e.g., maleic anhydride grafted polypropylene)
material must be available in grades suitable for extrusion (no cross‑linking or curing required)
cost and availability acceptable for lab‑scale production

final_candidate.justification: DIC.PPS is a linear PPS grade with a glass transition temperature of ~280 °C and a Vicat softening point above 200 °C, satisfying the thermal requirement. PPS exhibits excellent resistance to acids, bases, hydrocarbons, and many organic solvents, matching the chemical compatibility criteria. It can be melt‑extruded at 280–300 °C and is compatible with co‑extrusion of TPU/TPE/TPV when a maleic anhydride grafted polypropylene compatibilizer is added; this has been demonstrated in multilayer film production (see patents Doc 1, Doc 2). DIC.PPS is PFAS‑free and commercially available for lab‑scale extrusion.

process_recipe.description: Dry feed preparation – dry both DIC.PPS pellets (≤5 % moisture) and TPU pellets; blend with 2–3 wt % maleic anhydride grafted polypropylene compatibilizer.
Primary extrusion – feed the blended hard layer (DIC.PPS + compatibilizer) into a single‑chamber extruder set to 290 °C, 200 rpm.
Secondary extrusion – feed the soft TPU layer into a second chamber of a co‑extruder set to 210 °C, 200 rpm.
Co‑extrusion – merge the two melt streams in a multi‑layer die to form a bilayer film or tube; cool rapidly in a water bath at ~30 °C.
Post‑processing – unwind, reheat if necessary for annealing (120 °C for 30 min), and cut to size.
